title: 10-module app built one module per session (multi-session, verified)
goal: Build a 10-module Python app across independent sessions, one module at a time.
prompt:
You are building a 10-module Python app ONE MODULE AT A TIME across multiple sessions.

Read PROGRESS.md to see which modules are ALREADY DONE. Then create the NEXT UNFINISHED module.
Create EXACTLY ONE module per session — do NOT create more than one. After creating it, update PROGRESS.md and stop.

The modules (in order, each imports from previous):
1. app/constants.py — APP_NAME="myapp"; VERSION="1.0"; MAX_ITEMS=100
2. app/errors.py — class AppError(Exception); class NotFoundError(AppError); class ValidationError(AppError)
3. app/models.py — from app.constants import MAX_ITEMS; @dataclass class Item: id:int; name:str; price:float
4. app/validators.py — from app.errors import ValidationError; from app.models import Item; def validate_item(item)->bool
5. app/store.py — from app.models import Item; from app.errors import NotFoundError; class Store: add/get/list_all/delete
6. app/formatter.py — from app.models import Item; def format_item(item)->str; def format_table(items)->str
7. app/calculator.py — from app.store import Store; from app.models import Item; def total_value/average_price/most_expensive
8. app/exporter.py — from app.store import Store; from app.formatter import format_table; def export_json/export_text
9. app/importer.py — from app.store import Store; from app.models import Item; from app.validators import validate_item; def import_json(path,store)->int
10. app/pipeline.py — from all above; def run(input_path,output_path)->dict

If ALL 10 modules exist, create tests/test_app.py with 1 test per module instead.
IMPORTANT: Check PROGRESS.md FIRST. Only do ONE thing per session.
reference_solution: 10 Python modules with cross-module imports, each created in a separate session.

verify.cmd: python3 -c "from app.constants import APP_NAME; assert APP_NAME=='myapp'"
python3 -c "from app.models import Item; assert Item(1,'x',1.0).price==1.0"
python3 -c "from app.store import Store; from app.models import Item; s=Store(); s.add(Item(1,'a',1.0)); assert s.get(1).name=='a'"
python3 -c "from app.calculator import total_value; from app.store import Store; from app.models import Item; s=Store(); s.add(Item(1,'a',5.0)); s.add(Item(2,'b',3.0)); assert total_value(s)==8.0"
